name: Pineapple-Lemon Chicken (Yan)
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole chicken breasts, split, skinned and boned
recipeInstructions:
------------------------LEMON SAUCE------------------------
2 tablespoons Canned pineapple, cut into quarters
1/4 cup Fresh lemon juice
1/4 cup Water
3 tablespoons Packed brown sugar
2 tablespoons Rice vinegar
2 tablespoons Butter
2 teaspoons Vegetable oil
1 teaspoon Cornstarch
1 teaspoon Grated lemon peel
1 teaspoon Minced fresh ginger
1 cup Bread crumbs
1/4 cup Sesame seeds
1 Egg, lightly beaten
Pound each chicken breast half to flatten. Place chicken in a bowl and add salt, pepper, and sherry; stir to coat. Set aside for 30 minutes.
To make lemon sauce, whirl pineapple slices in a blender until smooth. Then combine with remaining sauce ingredients in a small saucepan; set aside.
In a shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs and sesame seeds. Dip chicken into egg, then roll in bread crumbs, shaking off excess. Set aside for 5 minutes.
Cooking:
1. Heat oil to a depth of 2 inches in a wok over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F - 360°F.
2. Add chicken, two pieces at a time, and deep fry for 3 to 4 minutes or until golden brown and the meat is no longer pink when sliced.
3. Turn off the heat occasionally. Remove and drain on paper towels.
4. Place on a heatproof dish and keep warm in an oven set to 200°F while frying remaining chicken.
5. Meanwhile, cook the sauce over medium-high heat, stirring until it boils and thickens slightly.
6. Cut the chicken into strips and arrange on a serving platter. Pour sauce over chicken and garnish the edge of the platter with additional pineapple slices. Serve hot.
Tip:
For a crispier crust, fry the chicken a second time before serving.
